region_of_origin: India (North Indian / Punjabi); served in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Paneer Butter Masala; closest parent 'curry (food)' used as ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Paneer Butter Masala; A0CKP (Curry dishes) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Paneer Butter Masala or sufficiently specific parent curry dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Paneer Butter Masala is approximately 300 g, comprising cubed paneer in a rich tomato-butter-cream gravy, usually accompanied by bread or rice.

nutrient_source.title: Paneer Butter Masala — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for component cross-check
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) provides values for paneer-based curry dishes. Per-100 g values for Paneer Butter Masala are derived from ICMR-NIN data for paneer curry (shahi paneer / makhani-style) and cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy entries for paneer (FDC ID 173420) and tomato-cream curry sauce components. Energy calculated from macros using Atwater factors. Values represent a typical restaurant-style preparation with butter, cream, tomatoes, cashews, and spices.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson, Brand-Miller et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — paneer-based curry and mixed dairy-tomato dishes
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Paneer Butter Masala in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 35 based on analogous mixed dairy-tomato curry dishes. Paneer is a low-GI dairy food (GI ~25–30 per University of Sydney data for cheese/curd foods). The tomato-cream-butter gravy with cashews and spices contributes minimal glycemic carbohydrates. A composite GI of 35 is defensible and consistent with low-GI mixed curry dishes reported in Atkinson et al. (2021) Am J Clin Nutr. GL per serving = (35 × 6.8 × 3) / 100 = 7.1 for a 300 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg/100 g) and saturated fat from butter and cream
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced butter and cream preparation, as a full 300 g restaurant serving delivers approximately 960 mg sodium, approaching 40% of the recommended daily limit of 2400 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) due to high protein and fat content from paneer, butter, and cream moderating glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Paneer Butter Masala has a low glycemic index of approximately 35 and a moderate glycemic load of 7.1 per standard serving, making it a relatively suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed with a small portion of low-GI bread or rice and without added sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat content (~6.2 g/100 g) from butter, cream, and paneer contributing to LDL cholesterol elevation risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Paneer Butter Masala in moderation (≤150 g per occasion) due to its high saturated fat content; requesting preparation with reduced butter and substituting cream with low-fat yogurt can lower the saturated fat load by approximately 30–40%.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Paneer Butter Masala (also known as Paneer Makhani or Butter Paneer) is a classic North Indian Punjabi curry dish consisting of cubed paneer — a fresh, non-melting Indian cottage cheese — simmered in a rich, velvety sauce made from butter, tomatoes, onions, cashews, cream, and aromatic spices including cumin, coriander, garam masala, ginger, and garlic. The dish is characterised by its deep orange-red colour, creamy texture, and a mildly spiced, slightly sweet flavour profile. It is one of the most popular Indian restaurant dishes globally and is widely served in Singapore's Indian restaurants. Nutritionally, a 300 g restaurant serving provides approximately 450 kcal, 19.5 g protein, 33 g fat (of which ~18.6 g is saturated), and 20.4 g carbohydrate. Paneer is a significant source of calcium (~180 mg/100 g) and complete protein. The dish has a low glycemic index (~35) due to the high fat and protein content slowing glucose absorption, making it relatively suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. However, the high saturated fat and sodium content (approximately 960 mg per restaurant serving) warrant moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease or hypertension. The dish is naturally vegetarian, gluten-free, and halal, and is commonly served with naan, roti, or basmati rice.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Paneer Butter Masala?
Is Paneer Makhani good for diabetics?

Can I eat Paneer Butter Masala if I have high blood pressure?
meal_context: paired with naan, roti, or basmati rice

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) for paneer-based makhani-style curry dishes and cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy paneer component data. No single exact entry for 'Paneer Butter Masala' exists in ICMR-NIN; values represent a defensible adaptation from the closest published Indian curry dish data. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for comparable dairy-tomato curry dishes. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this dish category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this dish category. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for paneer and butter and are approximate. The food_name in the input JSON included '(Paneer 干酪)' which is a parenthetical Chinese gloss; the canonical English food_name has been normalised to 'Paneer Butter Masala'. The cuisine field in the input was 'Singapore' reflecting the serving context; region_of_origin correctly identifies North Indian / Punjabi origin while cuisine is set to 'Indian' as the culinary tradition. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Paneer Butter Masala was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. GL per serving calculated as (35 × 6.8 g carbohydrate × 300 g serving) / 100 = 7.14, rounded to 7.1.
